touches.ai_summary: Initial LinkedIn outreach to Grayson Kang introducing our CRM automation platform. Grayson responded expressing interest in streamlining their sales process and reducing manual data entry.

questions_asked.question: What kind of automation workflows does your platform support?
questions_asked.answer: We support unlimited automation workflows including lead routing, follow-up sequences, data enrichment, and custom triggers based on any CRM event or field change.

next_steps: Schedule intro call to discuss current pain points

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email sent with case study from similar managed IT company. Grayson replied confirming interest and sharing that their team currently spends 8-10 hours weekly on manual CRM updates.

questions_asked.question: Can your system integrate with our existing help desk software?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we have pre-built integrations with all major help desk platforms plus a flexible API for custom connections.

next_steps: Grayson to share current tech stack details

touches.ai_summary: Sent discovery questionnaire and calendar link. Grayson responded with completed questionnaire highlighting needs around lead follow-up automation and reporting visibility. Noted they have 5 sales reps who would use the system.
next_steps: Schedule discovery call for next week

touches.ai_summary: Discovery call with Grayson focused on current workflow pain points. OrbitLens currently using basic CRM with heavy manual processes. Team losing deals due to slow follow-up times. Grayson mentioned Aaliyah Schmidt (IT Director) will need to approve any new software.

questions_asked.question: What's your current process for following up on inbound leads?
questions_asked.answer: Mostly manual - reps check the CRM a few times a day and create tasks. We're missing leads that come in after hours.

questions_asked.question: How quickly can we get this set up if we move forward?
questions_asked.answer: Typical implementation is 2-3 weeks including data migration and workflow configuration.

next_steps: Send demo invite including Aaliyah Schmidt

touches.ai_summary: Demo session rescheduled from previous week. Walked through core automation workflows and reporting dashboards. Grayson engaged and asked detailed questions about specific use cases. Noted that only Grayson attended - Aaliyah was unable to join.

questions_asked.question: Can we set different automation rules for different lead sources?
questions_asked.answer: Absolutely - you can create unlimited conditional workflows based on any combination of fields including lead source, company size, industry, etc.

questions_asked.question: What kind of training is included?
questions_asked.answer: We provide live onboarding sessions for admins and end users, plus ongoing access to our training portal and support team.

next_steps: Grayson to brief Aaliyah on platform capabilities

touches.ai_summary: Exec meeting with Grayson to discuss business case and ROI. Reviewed time savings projections and impact on sales velocity. Grayson mentioned budget constraints and asked about pricing flexibility. Still no executive sponsor engaged from their leadership team.

questions_asked.question: What's the typical ROI timeline your customers see?
questions_asked.answer: Most customers see measurable time savings within the first month and positive ROI within quarter one, primarily from increased conversion rates and rep productivity.

questions_asked.question: Who else should be involved in evaluating this from your leadership team?
questions_asked.answer: It's mostly my call on sales tools. I'll keep our COO in the loop but he trusts my judgment on these decisions.

objections: Budget is tight this quarter - we're looking at several tools and need to prioritize
next_steps: Send formal proposal with ROI breakdown

touches.ai_summary: Proposal review meeting with Grayson. Discussed pricing at $3,900 ACV for 5 seats with unlimited automation tier. Grayson expressed concern about the investment level and requested significant discount to fit within approved budget.

questions_asked.question: Is there a lower tier we could start with and upgrade later?
questions_asked.answer: The unlimited automation tier is really the best fit for your use case - the basic tier would limit you to 10 workflows which wouldn't support your needs.

questions_asked.question: What kind of pricing flexibility do you have?
questions_asked.answer: Let me discuss with my manager what we can do to work within your budget constraints.

objections: The pricing is higher than what we budgeted for this initiative
next_steps: Grayson to confirm exact budget parameters

touches.ai_summary: Negotiation meeting to discuss revised pricing. Presented 23.5% discount to bring deal within budget. Grayson appreciative of the flexibility and indicated this would work. Discussed implementation timeline and contract terms.

questions_asked.question: Can we do a month-to-month agreement instead of annual?
questions_asked.answer: The discounted pricing requires an annual commitment, but we can include a 30-day out clause if the platform doesn't meet expectations after onboarding.

questions_asked.question: What's your timing to get this implemented?
questions_asked.answer: Ideally we'd start implementation within the next two weeks so we can have it running before end of quarter.

next_steps: Grayson to route for internal approval

touches.ai_summary: Final pricing call to confirm contract details and answer remaining questions. Grayson confirmed the discounted pricing works and he's ready to move forward pending final sign-off. Discussed payment terms and implementation kickoff scheduling.

questions_asked.question: Can we do net-30 payment terms?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we can accommodate net-30 for the initial invoice.

questions_asked.question: When can we schedule the kickoff call with Aaliyah's team?
questions_asked.answer: We can get that scheduled within 48 hours of contract signature - I'll coordinate directly with Aaliyah on her availability.
